entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON (SOC)
entities.description: The amount of organic carbon stored in the soil, used as an indicator of soil health and carbon sequestration.

entities.name: MICROBIAL BIOMASS C (MBC)
entities.description: The carbon contained within the living microbial biomass of the soil.

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested per unit area.

entities.name: VERTIC INCEPTISOLS
entities.description: A specific type of soil characterized by high shrink-swell clay content.

entities.name: SEMI ARID CONDITION
entities.description: Environmental conditions characterized by low and irregular rainfall.

entities.name: CENTRAL PENINSULAR INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the study was conducted.

entities.name: IMPROVED MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Agricultural practices including broadbed-and-furrow landform, composted crop residues, and Gliricidia sepium pruning.

entities.name: TRADITIONAL MANAGEMENT
entities.description: Agricultural practices consisting of sowing on grade land form without organic nutrient additions.

entities.name: SEQUENTIAL CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops in sequence on the same field in a year.

entities.name: BROADBED-AND-FURROW
entities.description: A landform management technique used to improve water management and soil structure.

entities.name: COMPOSTED CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: The application of decomposed plant materials to the soil to provide nutrients.

entities.name: GLIRICIDIA SEPIUM
entities.description: A nitrogen-fixing tree used as a hedge and for pruning to add organic matter to the soil.

entities.name: GRADE LAND FORM
entities.description: A traditional flat land preparation method for sowing.

entities.name: SOIL CARBON SEQUESTRATION
entities.description: The process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon dioxide in the soil.
